title: Maximum-value dependency-closed selection (max-weight closure via min-cut)
source: Maximum-weight closure: pick a subset maximizing total (possibly negative) value, closed under 'a requires b' constraints. The non-obvious observation is the reduction to a minimum s-t cut (project-selection / max-weight-closure); the natural 'take all positives + close' greedy is wrong. Ground truth: independent brute-force subset enumeration (small n) AND an independent Edmonds-Karp max-flow oracle (medium n, where brute is infeasible).
